headline: The best cheesecake in the world... probably contains sake
summary: And other ways to cook with Japanese wine
body_text:
Food writer Emiko Davies makes a ricotta cheesecake that may be the best introduction to the use of sake as an ingredient in cooking. It may also be the best cheesecake you ever eat.

The recipe appears in Davies’ new book The Japanese Pantry (Smith Street Books), which looks at seven essentials of Japanese cuisine including miso, soy sauce and sesame. As well as being a national drink, sake is a key component in cooking and fixture of special occasions such as New Year when a cup of spiced sake (otoso) ushers in good health.

“In Japan, there is an ancient proverb, ‘sake is the best medicine of all’, which reminds me of the Italian proverb, ‘good wine makes good blood’,” says Davies. “Like miso and soy, making sake involves the most Japanese of ingredients: rice and koji, transformed by fermentation. But compared to miso and soy, sake’s flavour is so delicate. It is pure umami, enhancing the flavours of everything else in the dish with such subtlety.”

Davies’ cheesecake recipe actual
